title: Hajós' Conjecture (k=5)
statement_text: Every $5$-chromatic graph contains a subdivision of $K_{5}$.
context_text: Hajós conjectured that every $k$-chromatic graph contains a subdivision of $K_k$. For $k=4$ this is classical (Dirac); for $k\geq 7$ it is false (Catlin 1979; Erdős–Fajtlowicz 1981); the $k=5$ case remains open. The abstract explicitly states that the paper's main theorem makes progress towards this conjecture on $K_5$-subdivisions in 5-chromatic graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves that every 5-chromatic graph contains a dominating $K_{5}$-model $(T_1,\dots,T_5)$ with $T_4=\{v\}$ and $T_5=\{w\}$ singletons and $v,w$ adjacent, providing structural progress strictly stronger than the known $K_5$-minor result and contributing towards the existence of a $K_5$-subdivision in every 5-chromatic graph.
notes: Verbatim statement of the conjecture is not given in the provided paper text (only named in the abstract as 'Hajós' conjecture on $K_5$-subdivisions in 5-chromatic graphs'); standard formulation used.
